channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: White leads No. 22 Illinois to 109-77 blowout at No. 9 Oregon
item.description: Tre White had 20 points and 11 rebounds as No. 22 Illinois made a resounding statement on the road with a 109-77 blowout of ninth-ranked Oregon.
item.dc:creator: Steve Mims, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tom Hoge leads at Kapalua where good golf exceeds expectations in PGA Tour opener
item.description: KAPALUA, Hawaii (AP) — Tom Hoge grew up in North Dakota and found the ideal vibe for Kapalua on Thursday, keeping expectations low and riding the momentum of good golf on his way to a 9-under 64 to take a one-shot lead at The Sentry in the PGA Tour season opener.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Postponed Sugar Bowl played with enhanced security about 36 hours after terror attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Georgia and Notre Dame fans packed a plaza adjacent to the Superdome and enjoyed music under clear skies — under the watch of snipers on rooftops — before filtering into the stadium for Thursday's College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl. “It was a lot of fun.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Big Will's Week 18 NFL Picks
item.description: In this edition of NFL picks, Big Will focuses on the final AFC wildcard spot as three teams fight for the 7-seed.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Big 12 commissioner calls for 'national standards' for calling targeting after controversial play
item.description: Big 12 commissioner Brett Yormark is calling for more consistency on what constitutes a targeting penalty after no call was made on Texas safety Michael Taaffe’s high hit on Arizona State receiver Melquan Stovall late in the College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Peach Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Surging Ohio State to face tenacious Texas in the Cotton Bowl for a spot in the CFP title game
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Texas will be a whole lot closer to home than Ohio State in the Cotton Bowl. The Buckeyes appear to be a whole lot closer to Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Notre Dame draws back-to-back running into the kicker penalties to open Sugar Bowl
item.description: The Notre Dame Fighting Irish got off to a rough start in the Sugar Bowl against the Georgia Bulldogs in a CFP quarterfinal game Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donate blood in January for chance to win trip for 2 to Super Bowl LIX
item.description: The American Red Cross and the NFL are offering two tickets to the Super Bowl to encourage people to donate blood this month.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Clarke, Dane Kelly, Local 4

item.title: Superdome welcomes Sugar Bowl fans after New Orleans terror attack
item.description: College football fans flocked to the Caesars Superdome on Thursday afternoon to get ready for the Sugar Bowl after it was postponed due to the New Orleans terror attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big Game Bound: The Battle for the NFC Throne
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS – The final week of the NFL regular season begins with playoff futures and draft positions still to be decided. Twelve teams have already punched their postseason tickets with the Kansas City Chiefs earning the #1 seed and 1st round bye in the AFC.
item.dc:creator: Brett Bensley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bears CB Jaylon Johnson earns team's lone Pro Bowl selection this season, his 2nd straight
item.description: Bears cornerback Jaylon Johnson has been selected to represent the NFC at the 2025 Pro Bowl Games, the NFL announced Thursday morning. The games will be held a week before the Super Bowl, on Sunday, Feb. 2, at Camping World Stadium in Orlando, Fla.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Pistons guard Jaden Ivey sustains leg injury in victory over the Magic
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Detroit guard Jaden Ivey sustained what appeared to be a severe injury to his lower left leg in the fourth quarter of Detroit's victory over the Orlando Magic on Wednesday night. Orlando's Cole Anthony slipped and fell as he and Ivey went for the ball.
item.dc:creator: DAVE HOGG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bills clinch the AFC's No. 2 seed with a 40-14 rout of the undisciplined Jets
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Josh Allen reflected on Buffalo’s many offseason critics and doubters when asked about the Bills achieving their latest milestones while sewing up the AFC’s No. 2 seed with a 40-14 rout of the unraveling and undisciplined New York Jets on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz lose to Sixers for sixth straight home loss
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Joel Embiid and Tyrese Maxey each scored 32 points to lead the Philadelphia 76ers to a 114-111 victory over the Utah Jazz on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Southern California overcomes 17-point deficit to beat Texas A&M 35-31 in Las Vegas Bowl
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The Southern California Trojans ended their season the way they started it. On Sept. 1 at Allegiant Stadium in the opener, the Trojans overcame a third-quarter deficit and scored with eight seconds left to beat LSU 27-20.
item.dc:creator: W.G. RAMIREZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Shaq Barrett expected to re-sign with Bucs after being waived by Dolphins, reports say
item.description: Former Tampa Bay Buccaneers linebacker Shaq Barrett is expected to once again sign with the Bucs after he was waived by the Miami Dolphins, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5
